messages.content:
I can’t execute tests from here. To run "load test task 8" I need to know which load testing tool and where the task is defined (project repo, CI job name, or test file). Below are concise, practical run instructions for common setups — pick the one that matches your project or tell me which you use and I’ll tailor the steps.

Reasoning: different tools use different commands and output formats, so use the exact command for your tool and capture the results.

Common commands and tips
- k6 (JS test file tests/task8.js)
  - Run: k6 run tests/task8.js
  - Headless with env vars: TEST_TASK=8 k6 run tests/task8.js
  - Save metrics: k6 run --out json=task8_results.json tests/task8.js
- Locust (locustfile.py with a task id or specific class)
  - Headless: locust -f locustfile.py --headless -u 100 -r 10 -t 10m --run-time 10m --csv=task8
  - If task selection needs an env var: TASK_ID=8 locust -f locustfile.py --headless ...
- Artillery (YAML scenario file task8.yml)
  - Run: artillery run task8.yml
  - Save report: artillery run task8.yml -o task8_report.json
  - Generate HTML: artillery report --output task8_report.html task8_report.json
- JMeter (JMX file task8.jmx)
  - Non-GUI: jmeter -n -t task8.jmx -l task8_results.jtl -j task8_jmeter.log
  - Convert to CSV / HTML report as needed.
- pytest-based load tests (maybe using pytest + locust or custom)
  - Run a specific test file: pytest tests/test_load_task8.py -q
  - Use markers: pytest -m load -k task8
- npm script / Makefile / Docker
  - npm: npm run load:task8 or npm run load -- --task=8 (check package.json)
  - Makefile: make load TASK=8
  - Dockerized test: docker-compose run --rm loadtester sh -c "run-command-for-task-8"
- CI job (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI)
  - Trigger the pipeline/job that runs the load test task (use the CI web UI or API). Provide job name or pipeline ID if you want exact API calls.

What to capture and share
- Summary metrics: requests/sec, p95/p99 latency, error rate, throughput, total requests, CPU/memory of target.
- Raw results file (JSON/CSV/JTL) and any generated HTML report.
- If a test fails or shows high errors, include the log lines with errors and the load generator system metrics.

How I can help next
- Tell me: which tool is used, where the task file is (file name or CI job name), and whether you want a single run or a parameter sweep (concurrency/duration).
- I can give the exact command, explain how to interpret results, or provide a script to automate runs and collect metrics.
- If you want me to modify test code or CI config, submit again with a concrete coding request (or turn off Chat-only mode).

nextActions.label: Continue chat
nextActions.description: Ask a follow-up question in the same thread.
